understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to clear each level by getting every goal tile marked '+' to be occupied at the same time by selectable pieces. When all goals in the current level were covered simultaneously, the level would report WIN; completing all levels would win the whole run within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels per cell) in that direction, with collisions governed by nearby objects and boundaries. If an adjacent object in the movement direction was pushable (e.g., a block), the move could push it along provided space allowed; walls or boundaries could block movement, sometimes with non-uniform behavior depending on wall type/edge interactions. When multiple selectable pieces existed, CLICK with an exact on-screen/pixel coordinate of another selectable switched control to that piece; the semantic grid distinguished the controlled piece (P) from other selectables (S), and the movement_blocked flags were only hints about adjacency rather than guarantees that a move would fail.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The objective was to clear each level by getting every goal tile marked '+' to be occupied at the same time by selectable pieces. When all goals in the current level were covered simultaneously, the level would report WIN; completing all levels would win the whole run within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels per cell) in that direction, with collisions governed by nearby objects and boundaries. If an adjacent object in the movement direction was pushable (e.g., a block), the move could push it along provided space allowed; walls or boundaries could block movement, sometimes with non-uniform behavior depending on wall type/edge interactions. When multiple selectable pieces existed, CLICK with an exact on-screen/pixel coordinate of another selectable switched control to that piece; the semantic grid distinguished the controlled piece (P) from other selectables (S), and the movement_blocked flags were only hints about adjacency rather than guarantees that a move would fail.
